title: helianthemum canum f. glandulosum
intro: Helianthemum canum f. glandulosum, also known as the sticky rockrose or glandular frostweed, is a perennial plant species within the Helianthemum canum complex, characterized by its yellow flowers and sticky leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Helianthemum canum f. glandulosum include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
